item.title: Loved it
item.content:encoded:
This is exactly the kind of SCP article I like. Creepy around the edges, funny in the middle. I was confused by the mushroom connection at first, then the end tied it all up nicely.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you ask me, I LOVE it. It's weird and funny and scary and sad all at once, I love the Rob Cantor song, I think Shia Labaeiouf is… an actor. I just think it could be improved a bit by diving deeper into what's up with that mushroom stuff, even with the experiment log going into it as much as it does.

Also, this is EXTREMELY memeworthy, somebody should get on that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may be my most disliked SCP so far. The underlying concept is both fascinating and horrifying but between the absurdity factor and density of text I just couldn't stand it. An extreme excess of LaBeouf -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: this comment was unnecessary
cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the kind of article that freaks me out. Not because of the Shia LaBeouf element, of course, but the idea of suddenly and inexplicably developing an anomalous condition that ruins your life like that. It pushes the same fear button as hearing about a car accident that results in paralysis, despite the obvious absence of the knowledge that it could happen to me.

I feel like they should at least try to remove the weird fungus in the guy's head to a) see if that fixes it and b) study the stuff up close. I also wonder about the size/shape issue and how it appears to observers. If they somehow convinced the guy to ride a horse, would they then see him "sinking into" the horse due to the size difference? Or in a case involving a smaller animal, like the example where he dragged a worm by its "arm", what if somebody put their hand in the area where he sees Shia LaBeouf's body? Would he see the person waving their arm around inside the body like a ghost? And seconding Westrin's question above; I can see why they haven't tested it on a cellular level. (I don't think any microorganisms are Animalia, so they wouldn't trigger the effect, but a cell from an animal presumably would. (And if I'm wrong about the taxonomy thing, insert "this is why I don't write SCPs" joke here.))

Also, I wonder if he could eat meat properly if they blindfolded him. "The true nature of SCP-1247's meals was revealed to it only once the bite had been taken." — that implies that the perception effect is tied to the sense of sight, or at least doesn't apply to taste. It seems to apply to touch, but in all of the tests, he was able to see the thing he was touching. If you gave him a hamburger and he couldn't see it, would it feel like a normal, properly-sized hamburger to him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great article, but I have one question: What would happen if he looked at a micro-organism under a microscope? Would he perceive the Shia Lebeouf to "break" the microscope, due to Shia's size?

Better yet, what would happen if he looked at it through the micro-scope, and grabbed the perceived Shia's arm? Would he be "carrying" a micro-organism?

Still, great article, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fucking love this and can't believe I've only just discovered this. +1 forever.

item.title: Anti-LaBeouf Glasses?
item.content:encoded:
It says 1247's primary anomalous effect doesn't extend to objects viewed througha camera,, does this mean if they gave the guy a headset like a SCRAMBLE kit he'd be able to perceive things normally?
+1 by the way, this is actually scarier the more I think about it. I would go insane.

item.title: Re: THis srsly needs J designation
item.content:encoded: I feel that with all the weirdness in the SCP foundation universe, Pop culture can be a valid source of anomalies. The blending together of the Mundane and the weird. Waking up one day with this dudes situation would really suck.

item.title: Re: THis srsly needs J designation
item.content:encoded:
it's not scary?

imagine if you were unable to ever look at another person
or animal
or bug
or even a piece of meat

because they all looked the same

regardless of who they looked like

imagine if everyone in the world, except you, was the same person

and that has no creep factor?

not even when there's an extradimensional fungus god living in your brain, and eating you alive a tiny bit at a time?

item.title: THis srsly needs J designation
item.content:encoded: I gave it a down vote. A pop culture entry meant to be funny. Their is no creep factor in it. Its not scary, IT doesn't provoke any deep ideas. Its meant to be a joke and should be treated as such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expected to downvote this because it's such a silly idea, but you actually managed to pull it off. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I never realized, but that actors name gets funnier the more you say it in its entirety

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://loweringthebar.net/2016/04/good-reason-to-kill-60-looked-like-shia.html

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus Christ, the amount of discussion this has caused is mind boggling! Despite if the original author was planning on making an awesome SCP or not, they succeeded in making one that literally everyone wants to talk about!
Congrats to you sir or madam, +1 for absolute strangeness and discomfort while reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What?
+1

Although that mushroom thing really did seem tacked on and added nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: must have missed that part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't think the concept passes muster for me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
holes must have been a pretty damn bad movie for shia to appear everywhere

or a really good movie

?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so glad I found this so I don't have to repost the same thought.

My thinking? He starts seeing dead Shia Laboeufs. Nothing but dead Shias. That would probably wreak havoc on his psychological state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This guy can also drag a blue whale like it's no heavier than a person, pick up a worm by an arm it doesn't have and see through animals if they're longer than Shia LaBeouf. So, it's definitely anomalous, it's just a matter of debate whether it's dumb or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted because apart from the cerebrum, this is just some person who may or may not have a non-anomalous mental disorder saying "no really, everything looks like that actor.". The tests don't seem to prove it either way, and in the absence of any proof that this isn't just someone taking the piss, this boils down to the Foundation taking someone's word for it.

Happy to reconsider if what I've said can be demonstrated to be cobblers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two words after reading this: Good God

This has done something I'm not entirely sure I can understand the depth of.

Upvoted

item.title: How the hell is this on the site?
(no title)
item.content:encoded: FTC or FRC? Or some other competition?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man… The sheer amount of rare sentences in this…

only to discover that both of the officers present were Shia LaBeouf

whole, uncooked instances of Shia LaBeouf

as well as the constant trauma of being surrounded by supposedly-demonic Shia LaBeoufs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My reaction:

CONFUSION

(reads more)

SLIGHTLY LESS CONFUSION, BUT QUESTIONS

(reads testing log)

QUESTIONS ANSWERED. STILL CONFUSION.

+1 WHILE STILL YELLING.

item.title: Re: Explanations
item.content:encoded: Rob Cantor uploaded "Actual Cannibal Shia LaBeouf" to SoundCloud on March 3, 2012. This skip was published on 25 November 2012.

item.title: Re: Explanations
item.content:encoded: What's the song this was inspired by? I know there's a song called Shia Laboeuf and it's pretty popular, but it only came out six months ago as far as I'm aware.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely not a -J. The implications here are terrifying, and I couldn't help but run through the events of the SCP's recovery and subsequent experimentation presented as a short film in my head. It strikes me as the type of thing Shia Labeouf would actually act in. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've seen that. We loop it at my school robotics meets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… is this the guy being narrated in "Actual Cannibal Shia Labeouf"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shia LaBeouf is right behind you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kept reading in the vain hope that there would be something that made sense, but no, there was only Shia.

take my +1, you monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because count me among the crowd who did so due to the sheer what-the-fuckery implied in this. Much like Dexanote, my first thought was "the FUCK". Second thought was "+1".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…What? I can't. I can't give a vote.

On the one hand, you've achieved a very high level of mind-screwery on me and I commend that.

On the other hand, the mind-screwery was so high that I was more confused and dazed than horrified or amused.

I don't know if this was a good thing or bad. Well done though if your objective was to leave me stare in disbelief at my screen as I try to take in what I just read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1247 has been neutralized
– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though we can't prove he sees Shia, we can still see him interacting with organisms as if they were LaBeuof (moving an earthworm by grabbing an invisible arm), and this addressed while describing the second anomalous property.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh huh. I have a BIG problem with this article. Big.

How do we know he's not just crazy?

item.title: Re: Cross-testing
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1359: Immune! Pig_catapult has implied the effect to be related to her appearance. This is one human 1247 won't be cannibalizing!

SCP-247: Half-immune! The memetic effect is based on perception — maybe something to do with the pattern of the tiger's stripes? It wouldn't work in person. But hold on, the effect also works through photographs, which 1247 does not affect. However, seeing as he lacks a standard human brain, I'm calling that he's probably immune to a lot of memetic items. In a photo, he will see the tiger! Not immune to the psychic effect, though — if they meet face-to-face, he'll perceive a harmless Shia.

SCP-1177: Not immune! (mune?) Normal people still recognize her chopped bits as chopped bits, despite accepting them as currency. Likewise, 1247 will readily trade goods and services for an appropriate amount of Shia LaBeoufs.

The Toaster is not an animal!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird, funny, creepy. Surely, that's a big fat upvote from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [Couldn't Delete]

item.title: Cross-testing
item.content:encoded:
is 1247 immune to the effect of SCP-1359?

of SCP-247? SCP-1177? Me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand how this guy could have survived in a forest by hunting Shias. The article establishes that he even perceives insects and worms as life-sized copies of Shial Labeouf. Forests are full of insects, just ridiculous amounts of them. Shouldn't he see nothing but hundreds if not thousands of Shias scampering across the ground, climbing the trees and buzzing through the air every time he stepped outside his cabin? It'd be almost impossible to find actual animals hidden in the crowds of bug-Shias and none of them would have provided him with any sustenance because when they are in his mouth they turn back into normal bugs. Hell wouldn't he mostly be gettings mouthfuls of air every time he bites a Shia since the perceived Shias are much larger than the actual insects?

At least if he stayed in the city he could still go to the grocery store and buy fruits and vegetables and survive off a non-meat diet. In the forest he'd starve unless he's also growing his own gardens and canning veggies for the winter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does extend. So he sees Shias.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"However, SCP-1247's primary anomalous effect does extend to any animals observed in a reflection or through a lens."

Wait does this mean that if he's wearing a pair of glasses he sees things normally?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just so bizarre that it's beautiful. Gives off a heavy "Actual Cannibal Shia LeBeouf" feel. I don't really have any gripes with it.
+1 to you, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I'm hoping, HOPING that someone out there makes a mod of GTA V where every animal model is replaced with Shia LaBoeuf.

Or even better: TOKYO JUNGLE! YES! *u*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, ksaid, because I really respect you and you seem to thrive off this reaction…WHAT THE FUCK?

Honestly though this is fan-tucking-fastic. It made me laugh so hard people in the library are giving me weird looks. Take my +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the best thing I've ever read ever, which makes it my new favorite SCP.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I'm genuinely surprised the man hasn't tried to kill off the entire human race.

Since there hasn't been a proper post in this thread with the appropriate hat…

SHIA'S EVERYWHERE

IN THE OCEAN, WITH ANENOME SHIAS KEEPING THE CLOWNFISH SHIAS SAFE

IN THE AIR, SOARING MAJESTICALLY IN A V-FORMATION WHILE HONKING

EVERYFUCKINGWHERE, SUCKING EVERYONE'S BLOOD AND BEING A GENERAL SUMMER NUISANCE

item.content:encoded:
wat the shit

i mean the kfh;l jmklfhm;slsgf/s.

what?

WHAT THE FUCK IS THIS SHIT.

this some crazy fhosklsjklkldskhlkldkldk……………………….. i dont know.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TESTING TIME

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit, people in the office are gonna start looking at me funny…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely love this. Upvoted at the flocks of flying Shia LeBoeufs, and the fact that the instances all look like the current Shia is horrifying when you realise that if Shia dies, this poor guy is going to see corpses everywhere…

item.content:encoded:

What what what what what

What the actual fuck is this i dont even

…

Upvoted
